title: Clean multi-worker CLI output with per-worker spinner lines
description: With --max-workers > 1, the CLI run command interleaves spinner output from concurrent workers, producing garbled terminal output. Fix by adding a threading lock to ConsoleReporter for thread safety, and a new SpinnerPool class in console.py that reserves N terminal lines (one per max_workers) and updates each in place using ANSI cursor positioning. Update CliSchedulerReporter in cli.py to accept max_workers and use SpinnerPool when > 1, keyed by bead_id. Single-worker mode (max_workers=1) should behave identically to current. Non-TTY fallback should print lines sequentially without cursor control.
acceptance_criteria: All stdout.write calls in ConsoleReporter are protected by a threading lock
A SpinnerPool class exists that manages N concurrent spinner lines using ANSI cursor positioning
CliSchedulerReporter uses SpinnerPool when max_workers > 1, keyed by bead_id
Single-worker mode (max_workers=1) behaves identically to current
Non-TTY environments fall back to sequential line output

handoff_summary.completed: All acceptance criteria met: (1) ConsoleReporter.emit and Spinner._render/_finish protected by threading.Lock; (2) SpinnerPool class manages N concurrent spinner lines using ANSI cursor positioning; (3) CliSchedulerReporter uses SpinnerPool when max_workers > 1, keyed by bead_id; (4) Single-worker mode (max_workers=1) unchanged — uses original Spinner; (5) Non-TTY fallback prints sequential lines without cursor control; (6) All 111 existing tests pass.
handoff_summary.risks: ANSI cursor positioning assumes VT100-compatible terminals. Terminals with very narrow widths may truncate spinner labels. The fallback slot=0 overwrite when all slots are full is a safety net but would indicate max_workers misconfiguration.
handoff_summary.next_action: Test and review the SpinnerPool behavior with actual concurrent workers, and add unit tests for the new classes.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: cli.py:CliSchedulerReporter and command_run are modified — sibling beads touching scheduler reporter flow may conflict.

conflict_risks: cli.py:CliSchedulerReporter and command_run are modified — sibling beads touching scheduler reporter flow may conflict.

execution_history.summary: Implemented thread-safe multi-worker CLI output. Added threading lock to ConsoleReporter (protects all emit/write calls), created SpinnerPool class with ANSI cursor positioning for N concurrent spinner lines, and wired CliSchedulerReporter to use SpinnerPool when max_workers > 1. Single-worker mode is unchanged. Non-TTY falls back to sequential lines.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run only the specific test files directly related to your changes, not the full test suite. Use `uv run python -m unittest tests.<module_name> -v` to target individual test files rather than `discover`. Leave comprehensive test validation to the tester agent.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
